body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:model-select

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it

opencode supports per-turn model selection (capabilities.json `multi_model_orchestration: true`). The CLI exposes `-m, --model <provider/model>` on `opencode run` (verified live: `opencode run --help` lists the flag), and the in-TUI `/model` picker swaps the active model mid-session. So the agent CAN run turn 1 on model A and a later turn on model B within one `--session <id>` chain — the primitive the scenario exercises.

### daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store, and the model lives on `message.data.model.modelID` per message. The driver's transcript export already pulls `_model` via `json_extract(m.data,'$.model.modelID')` (drive-opencode-interactive.sh export SQL), so a genuine per-turn model change WOULD be observable — model B would land on the later turn's `_model` and the replay summary's `model_name` would follow latest-wins. The daemon is not the blocker.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: model-select)

The scenario requires the SAME session to run model A on one turn and model B on a later turn, with the change observed per turn. The opencode interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh`) drives opencode through headless `opencode run` subprocesses and implements ONLY three step types in its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch: `send`, `wait_turn` (a no-op — `opencode run` blocks until the turn ends), and `sleep`. There is NO step type that selects a model per send, and the `send` arm hardcodes the invocation as `opencode run --format default [--session <id>] -- "$text"` with NO `--model` flag — so every turn in a run uses the single config default (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` → `lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`). There is no driver primitive to switch the model mid-session.

### Ruled out: no send-only switch path (not a false gap)

- opencode's in-TUI `/model` picker is an arrow-key overlay — not drivable through the headless `opencode run` send path and the driver has no `keys` step anyway.
- `/model` as inline message text is NOT processed by headless `opencode run`. Probed live (2026-05-26, opencode + LM Studio default model): a `send`-path run of `opencode run -- "/model ionos/meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct"` created a session whose only message still carried `model.modelID = gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored` / `providerID = lmstudio` — i.e. the `/model` text was sent to the LLM as literal content; the model did NOT switch. So there is genuinely no send-only way to reach a second model.
- A second usable lmstudio model is also not declared in opencode.json (only `gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored` under the `lmstudio` provider); the `ionos` provider's models are reachable (IONOS_API_TOKEN is set) but only via the missing `--model` primitive, never via the send path.

## What would unblock it

Add a per-step model-selection primitive to `drive-opencode-interactive.sh` — e.g. a `send` step that accepts an optional `model` field threaded into `opencode run -m <provider/model>`, or a dedicated `model-select` step that sets the model for subsequent sends. Both reachable models (model A = config default `lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`; model B = a second reachable model, e.g. `ionos/<model>`) must be live on the box. Once the driver can run turn 1 on model A and turn 2 on model B in one `--session` chain, the cell becomes recordable: the daemon already reads per-turn model from the store, so the A→B switch would land at summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (latest-wins) — expected.jsonl carries only the two-turn lifecycle skeleton because the events.jsonl phase DSL has no model field.

## Confidence

0.85: the driver grammar (`send|wait_turn|sleep`, no `--model`) is read directly from the live driver source, the inline-`/model` no-op is proven by a live store probe, and the `-m/--model` flag is confirmed in `opencode run --help`. Not higher only because the unblocked recording itself (model A→B on one session) has not been captured — that is the developer task this gap defers.
caveats: Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode the agent supports model switching (`multi_model_orchestration: true`) and the daemon reads per-turn model from the SQLite store. The blocker is purely the driver's missing per-step model-selection primitive (gap:model-select).

recipe-lint.sh does NOT catch this gap: the gap is not a missing step TYPE (the recipe would use only send/wait_turn/sleep, all handled) but a missing model-selection CAPABILITY within the send arm — confirmed instead by live behavioral probing (inline /model is a no-op on headless `opencode run`).

sources.note: case "$TYPE" handles only send|wait_turn|sleep; run_send hardcodes `opencode run --format default [--session] -- text` with NO --model flag — no per-step model selection
exposes `-m, --model <provider/model>` — the flag exists on the agent, but the driver never passes it

resulting session message still carried model.modelID=gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored / providerID=lmstudio — inline /model is NOT processed by headless `opencode run`; it goes to the LLM as text and does not switch the model (rules out a false gap)
default model lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored; lmstudio provider declares only that one model; ionos models reachable (IONOS_API_TOKEN set) but only via the missing --model primitive
opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session/model state from SQLite, so a genuine per-turn switch WOULD be observable — daemon_capability: full
multi_model_orchestration: true (the primitive the scenario exercises)
